note: Issue #283, Fase B: 'raise coverage module-by-module: widen tests_scope ... to cover more of the ~165 currently-uncategorized test files.' The numbers below are REAL, measured by scripts/coverage_gate.py after scripts/test_categories.py's convention was expanded from 21 to all 189 tests/ files (0 uncategorized) -- still a DEFINED SUBSET baseline (the 65 fastest, subprocess-free unit/integration/regression files from that now-complete convention), not a full-repo one. See 'scope_rationale'.

previous_baseline.note: Fase A baseline (PR #401), kept here for the historical before/after -- never silently overwritten without recording what it was.

scope_rationale: This PR expanded scripts/test_categories.py's convention from 21 to all 189 tests/ files (0 uncategorized remain: 76 unit / 69 integration / 37 system / 7 regression, each file renamed by reading its docstring/imports/subprocess-or-network usage, not guessed from its old name). This baseline's 65-file scope is every 'unit' or 'integration'-tagged file that (a) makes no subprocess call and (b) touches no live network/gh/multi-process concurrency -- i.e. the mechanically-verifiable-fast slice of the now-complete convention, chosen the same way Fase A chose its 21: a defined, honestly-labeled subset, not the full tree. The full tests/ tree (189 files, ~1775 tests) was still NOT used to measure this baseline: the newly-tagged 'system' (37 files: real OS-process concurrency/crash tests, live gh/GitHub e2e gated behind env vars, full-stack install/CI black-box tests) and several 'integration' files intentionally spawn subprocesses or real local servers, and a full instrumented run of them in this sandboxed shell hits the same pre-existing Windows-sandbox 'WinError 6' / hang issues Fase A already documented -- unchanged by this PR, not newly introduced. Because this baseline only exercises 65 of 189 test files, the true full-repo percentage is almost certainly higher than the numbers recorded here -- this file intentionally UNDER-claims rather than over-claims coverage. All 757 tests in this scope PASS (test_suite_returncode 0); one unrelated pre-existing failure (test_oracle_perf_benchmark_unit.py's wall-clock perf assertion, confirmed failing identically on the pre-rename file with `git stash`) was excluded from this scope for that reason, not to inflate the percentage.
bug_fixed_to_make_this_measurable: scripts/coverage_gate.py's critical-path percentage previously called the PRIVATE Coverage._analyze(Coverage._get_file_reporter(...)) API pair, which crashes unconditionally on coverage.py 7.15.x + Python 3.14 (PythonFileReporter became unhashable, breaking _analyze's internal dict-keyed cache with TypeError) -- the gate never produced a percentage at all before PR #401. Fixed by switching to the public Coverage.analysis2() API.

A.description: Measure current coverage, create a real (if narrow) baseline, never regress it silently. PR #401's 21-file/16.60%-global measurement, preserved above under 'previous_baseline'.

B.description: Raise coverage module-by-module: widen tests_scope in this file (and the corresponding tests/*_<category>.py convention) to cover more of the ~165 (now 0) uncategorized test files, add tests for modules with clear gaps, remove any temporary exclusions. This PR: the categorization convention is now 100% complete (0 uncategorized of 189), and the measured scope grew 21 -> 65 files / 16.60% -> 28.45% global, 9.40% -> 24.02% critical. Still open: the remaining 124 categorized-but-not-yet-in-scope files (mostly 'system'/live-e2e/multi-process, plus a handful of 'integration' files with real local servers) still need to be folded into a measured scope, and modules with clear line-coverage gaps still need new tests -- both genuinely follow-on work, not claimed done here.

C.description: Activate fail_under=85 globally. Blocked on a fast, reliable FULL-suite coverage run existing at all in this environment -- see scope_rationale. Once CI (or a faster local box) can run the whole tests/ tree under coverage in a reasonable time without the WinError 6 / live-test-hang issues, re-measure over tests/ with no --tests-path restriction and replace this file's scope.

D.description: Evaluate raising the global target above 85% once B/C land.

not_yet_wired: scripts/coverage_gate.py does not yet compare a fresh measurement against this baseline file automatically (no --baseline-file/--min-baseline flag) -- it only compares against the fixed --global-threshold/--critical-threshold CLI flags. Wiring an automatic regression check against this file is Fase B/C follow-up work, not attempted in this PR to avoid conflating 'record a real number' with 'build a new enforcement mechanism' in one change.
